quran.text: So whoever does an atom's weight of good will see it.
And spend in the way of Allah and do not throw [yourselves] with your [own] hands into destruction [by refraining]. And do good; indeed, Allah loves the doers of good.

They ask you, [O Muhammad], what they should spend. Say, 'Whatever you spend of good is [to be] for parents and relatives and orphans and the needy and the traveler. And whatever you do of good - indeed, Allah is Knowing of it.'

The example of those who spend their wealth in the way of Allah is like a seed [of grain] which grows seven spikes; in each spike is a hundred grains. And Allah multiplies [His reward] for whom He wills. And Allah is all-Encompassing and Knowing.
Indeed, the men who practice charity and the women who practice charity and [they who] have loaned Allah a goodly loan - it will be multiplied for them, and they will have a noble reward.
And establish prayer and give zakah, and whatever good you put forward for yourselves - you will find it with Allah.
You will not attain righteousness until you spend from that which you love. And whatever you spend - indeed, Allah is Knowing of it.
And they give food in spite of love for it to the needy, the orphan, and the captive.
So as for the orphan, do not oppress [him]. And as for the petitioner, do not repel [him].
Believe in Allah and His Messenger, and spend out of that whereof He has made you successors. For those who believe among you and spend, for them is a great reward.
Those who spend their wealth in the night and in the day, secretly and publicly - they will have their reward with their Lord. And no fear will there be concerning them, nor will they grieve.
If you disclose your charitable expenditures, they are good; but if you conceal them and give them to the poor, it is better for you.
And cooperate in righteousness and piety, but do not cooperate in sin and aggression.
And in their wealth there is a right for the petitioner and the deprived.
And what will make you know what is the difficult path? It is the freeing of a slave, or feeding on a day of severe hunger an orphan of near relationship or a needy person in misery.
Allah will deprive usury of all blessing, but will give increase for deeds of charity.
Be steadfast in prayer and regular in charity. And whatever good ye send forth for your souls before you, ye shall find it with Allah.
And vie with one another for forgiveness from your Lord, and for a Paradise as wide as the heavens and the earth, prepared for the righteous. Those who spend in times of prosperity and adversity.

Righteousness is not that you turn your faces toward the east or the west, but [true] righteousness is [in] one who believes in Allah... and gives wealth, in spite of love for it, to relatives, orphans, the needy, the traveler, those who ask [for help], and for freeing slaves.
Satan threatens you with poverty and orders you to immorality, while Allah promises you forgiveness from Him and bounty. And Allah is all-Encompassing and Knowing.
And whatever you spend of anything, He will replace it. And He is the best of providers.
For them who have done good is the best [reward] and even more.
Take, [O, Muhammad], from their wealth a charity by which you purify them and cause them increase.
And as for the man who is a miser and thinks himself self-sufficient, and denies the Best, We will indeed make smooth for him the path to Misery.
Does he not know that Allah sees?
And be patient, for indeed, Allah does not allow to be lost the reward of those who do good.

He who has given his wealth to purify himself, and has in his mind no favor from anyone for which a reward is expected, but only the desire to seek the Countenance of his Lord, the Most High, He surely will be pleased.
Is there any reward for good other than good?
And worship your Lord until there comes to you the certainty (death).
So fear Allah as much as you are able, and listen and obey and spend; it is better for your souls.
Have you seen the one who denies the final judgment? That is the one who repulses the orphan, and does not encourage the feeding of the poor.
And what you give in zakah, desiring the countenance of Allah - those are the multipliers.
Indeed, your Lord extends provision for whom He wills and restricts [it]. Indeed, He is ever, concerning His servants, Acquainted and Seeing.
And do not make your hand [as] chained to your neck or extend it completely and [thereby] become blamed and insolvent.
And give the relative his right, and the needy, and the traveler, and do not spend wastefully.
So let them worship the Lord of this House, Who has fed them, [saving them] from hunger and made them safe, [saving them] from fear.
Woe to every backbiter and slanderer, who collects wealth and [continuously] counts it. He thinks that his wealth will make him immortal.
Indeed, mankind was created anxious: When evil touches him, impatient, and when good touches him, withholding [of it].
O you who have believed, spend from the good things which you have earned and from that which We have produced for you from the earth.

And those who are patient, seeking the countenance of their Lord, and establish prayer and spend from what We have provided for them secretly and publicly and prevent evil with good - for those will be the final attainment.
The righteous will be in gardens and springs, accepting what their Lord has given them. Indeed, they were before that, doers of good.
So give the relative his right, and the needy, and the wayfarer. That is best for those who desire the countenance of Allah, and it is they who will be the successful.
Never will you attain the good [reward] until you spend [in the way of Allah] from that which you love.
And whatever you spend in the cause of Allah will be fully repaid to you, and you will not be wronged.
But the righteous one will avoid it - [He] who gives his wealth to purify himself.
And those in whose wealth there is a recognized right, for the petitioner and the deprived.
Indeed, Allah is with those who are righteous and those who are doers of good.
My mercy encompasses all things. So I will decree it for those who are righteous and give zakah and those who believe in Our signs.
And hasten to forgiveness from your Lord and a garden as wide as the heavens and earth, prepared for the righteous.

Say, 'Indeed, my Lord extends provision for whom He wills of His servants and restricts [it] for him. But whatever thing you spend [in His cause] - He will compensate it; and He is the best of providers.'
Help one another in acts of piety and righteousness. And do not help one another in acts of sinfulness and transgression.
The Night of Decree is better than a thousand months.
And seek with that which Allah has given you the home of the Hereafter, and do not forget your share of the world. And do good as Allah has done good to you.

hadith.text: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) was the most generous of people, and he was the most generous during Ramadan when Jibril met him. Jibril used to meet him every night in Ramadan to study the Qur'an, and the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) was more generous with good deeds than the blowing wind.

Whoever provides the food for a fasting person to break his fast with, then for him is the same reward as his (the fasting person's), without anything being diminished from the reward of the fasting person.
Umrah during Ramadan is equivalent to Hajj (in reward).
When a man dies, his deeds come to an end except for three things: Sadaqah Jariyah (ceaseless charity); a knowledge which is beneficial, or a virtuous descendant who prays for him.
Protect yourself from hell-fire even by giving a piece of a date as charity.
Charity does not decrease wealth, no one forgives another except that Allah increases his honor, and no one humbles himself for the sake of Allah except that Allah raises his status.
The believer's shade on the Day of Resurrection will be his charity.

A charity is due for every joint in each person on every day the sun comes up: to act justly between two people is a charity; to help a man with his mount, lifting him onto it or hoisting up his belongings onto it, is a charity; a good word is a charity; and removing a harmful thing from the road is a charity.
The upper hand is better than the lower hand. The upper hand is the one that gives, and the lower hand is the one that receives.
I and the person who looks after an orphan and provides for him, will be in Paradise like this,' putting his index and middle fingers together.
Every act of goodness is a charity.
Give charity without delay, for it stands in the way of calamity.
The best charity is to give water to drink.
He who is not merciful to others, will not be treated mercifully.
He is not a believer who eats his fill while his neighbor beside him is hungry.
The best of people are those who are most beneficial to people.
A man's true wealth is the good he does in this world.
The one who looks after a widow and a poor person is like a Mujahid (warrior) in the cause of Allah, or like the one who fasts all the day and stands in prayer all the night.
There is a reward for serving any living being.
Your smiling in the face of your brother is a charity.
Wealth is not in having many possessions. Rather, true wealth is the richness of the soul.
The most beloved of deeds to Allah are those that are most consistent, even if it is small.
Allah is in the aid of His servant as long as His servant is in the aid of his brother.
Whoever relieves a believer’s distress of the distressful aspects of this world, Allah will rescue him from a difficulty of the difficulties of the Hereafter.
Kindness is a mark of faith, and whoever is not kind has no faith.
Look at those who are beneath you and do not look at those who are above you, for it is more suitable that you do not belittle Allah's favor upon you.
There is no envy except in two cases: a man whom Allah has given wealth and he spends it in the right way, and a man whom Allah has given wisdom and he acts according to it and teaches it to others.
O son of Adam, spend, and I will spend on you.
The charity of a Muslim cools the heat of the grave for him, and the believer will be in the shade of his charity on the Day of Resurrection.
The one who strives to sponsor a widow and a needy person is like the one who strives in the cause of Allah.
No one has ever eaten a better food than what he has earned with his own hands.
A man's spending on his family is a charity.
Whoever is not grateful to the people, is not grateful to Allah.
Save yourself from Hell-fire even by giving half a date-fruit in charity.
Do not withhold your money, lest Allah withholds from you.

Every Muslim has to give in charity. The people asked, 'O Allah's Prophet! If someone has nothing to give, what will he do?' He said, 'He should work with his hands and benefit himself and also give in charity.'
The best charity is that which is practiced by a wealthy person. And start giving first to your dependents.
If anyone of you is pleased to be safe from the great distress of the Day of Resurrection, he should grant respite to the one in straitened circumstances or remit his debt.
Your body has a right over you, your eyes have a right over you, and your wife has a right over you.
Verily, Allah is gentle and loves gentleness in all matters.
Allah will not be merciful to those who are not merciful to mankind.
A man giving a dirham in his lifetime is better for him than giving one hundred dirhams at the time of his death.
A kind word is a form of charity.
Whoever plants a tree and a human being or an animal eats from it, it is a charity for him.
The search for knowledge is an obligation for every Muslim.
The best among you are those who have the best manners and character.
The parable of the believers in their affection, mercy, and compassion for each other is that of a body. When any limb aches, the whole body reacts with sleeplessness and fever.
There are two blessings which many people lose: (They are) health and free time for doing good.
He who sleeps on a full stomach whilst his neighbour is hungry is not one of us.
Avoid the seven great destructive sins... and devouring the property of an orphan.
The one who repents from sin is like one who has no sin.

scholars.text: Ramadan is a month whose beginning is mercy, whose middle is forgiveness, and whose end is freedom from the fire. Increase good deeds, for their reward is multiplied.
The fasting person has two moments of joy: one when he breaks his fast, and the other when he meets his Lord.
scholars.source: Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ), often quoted by scholars

scholars.text: Do not feel ashamed if the amount of charity is small, because to refuse the needy is an act of greater shame.
scholars.source: Ali ibn Abi Talib (R.A), Nahj al-Balagha, Saying 67

scholars.text: The richest of the rich is the one who is not a prisoner of greed.
scholars.source: Ali ibn Abi Talib (R.A), Nahj al-Balagha, Saying 371

scholars.text: If you are unable to do a good deed, then restrain yourself from doing a bad deed. That, too, is a charity.
What you spend on your family is a charity. And what you spend on your servant is a charity. And what you spend on yourself is a charity.
He who has a thousand friends has not a friend to spare, and he who has one enemy will meet him everywhere.
scholars.source: Ali ibn Abi Talib (R.A), 1000 Sayings of Ali

scholars.text: Knowledge is a treasure, and its key is the question.
Patience is a pillar of faith.
The world is a bridge, so pass over it, but do not build your dwelling on it.
scholars.source: Ascribed to Isa (Jesus), son of Maryam

scholars.text: The less you have, the more you give.
The most beloved of people to Allah is the one who is most beneficial to the people.
The tongue is a small thing, but it can kill a big man.
Acquire knowledge and learn tranquility and dignity.
Let your speech be good and your face be smiling, and you will be loved by the people more than he who gives them a gift.
He who loves the world and its people will be left with a legacy of sorrow. He who loves Allah and His people will be left with a legacy of joy.
The dunya (worldly life) is a carcass, and its seekers are dogs.
scholars.source: Ali ibn Abi Talib (R.A), Nahj al-Balagha

scholars.text: Do not let your difficulties fill you with anxiety, after all, it is only in the darkest nights that stars shine more brightly.
The disease of the heart is worse than the disease of the body.
The real dunya is the one you spend in the way of Allah.
If you want to be a king, then be a slave of Allah.
The heart of the ignorant is in his mouth, and the tongue of the wise is in his heart.
scholars.source: Ali ibn Abi Talib (R.A), Nahj al-Balagha, Saying 40

scholars.text: Be like a flower that gives its fragrance even to the hand that crushes it.
Sincerity is to forget the creation by constantly looking at the Creator.
The source of every good is the knowledge that the servant’s affair is entirely in the hands of Allah.
This worldly life is a dream, and the Hereafter is the awakening. In between is death. We are living in a dream that will soon end.
The cure for five things is five things: The cure for a hard heart is to stroke the head of an orphan...
The sign that Allah is pleased with a servant is that He makes him a source of meeting the needs of people.
The intelligent person is one who controls his own self and works for what is after death.
Contentment is a treasure that is never exhausted.
scholars.source: Proverb attributed to Ali ibn Abi Talib

scholars.text: The best richness is the richness of the soul.
Three things follow a deceased person: his family, his wealth, and his deeds. His family and wealth return, but his deeds remain.
The strong man is not the good wrestler; the strong man is only the one who controls himself when he is angry.
How strange is the affair of the believer, for his affair is all good, and this applies to no one but the believer.
Whoever treads a path in search of knowledge, Allah will make easy for him the path to Paradise.
If you wish to know your status with Allah, then look at the status of Allah in your heart.
Patience is of two types: patience over what you dislike, and patience over what you love.
scholars.source: Ali ibn Abi Talib (R.A), Nahj al-Balagha, Saying 55

scholars.text: Our abode in this world is temporary, our life in it is a loan, our breaths are numbered and our indolence is manifest.
To be tested is to be purified.
The greatest jihad is to battle your own soul, to fight the evil within yourself.
True wealth is not in the possession of many things, but in being content with what one has.
The servant is not truly thankful to Allah unless he is thankful to people.
